data.title: Global oncology sector booming to $668B in 2034, with focus on GI tumors. 5 platforms leading the way.
data.description: A recent news commentary from Oncolytics Biotech Inc. reveals that the global oncology sector is projected to reach a staggering $668 billion by the year 2034. This substantial growth is attributed to a significant shift towards gastrointestinal cancers in the clinical field. The report emphasizes the increasing importance of research and development efforts in oncology, highlighting the potential for advancements in treating various types of cancer. This industry development signifies a promising future in cancer treatment and underscores the growing significance of addressing gastrointestinal cancers in the medical community.

data.title: CP Group unveiled a mixed-use master plan for the redevelopment of Piedmont Center, a 2.2M-SF property with 83,000 square feet.
data.description: In Atlanta, CP Group has revealed a mixed-use master plan for the redevelopment of Piedmont Center, increasing it to 83,000 square feet. This investment deepens the firm's involvement in Atlanta, as they plan to transform the 2.2 million square foot property into a premium mixed-use destination. CP Group, known for revamping the former CNN Center, is an owner-operator of office properties in the Sunbelt region. The master plan and rebranding were announced today.

data.title: Tompkins Finl (AMEX: TMP) Q4 earnings beat estimates with EPS of $1.78 vs $1.7. Revenue increased by $12.53M y-o-y.
data.description: Tompkins Finl (AMEX: TMP) released its Q4 earnings on Friday, January 30, 2026 at 09:00 AM. In the report, it was revealed that Tompkins Finl exceeded estimated earnings by 5.0%, with an EPS of $1.78 compared to the estimated $1.7. Additionally, the revenue saw an increase of $12.53 million from the previous year. Looking back at previous earnings records, the company had missed on EPS by... For the comprehensive story, please visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: ThinkMarkets wins Europe's Best Forex and CFD broker 2025 award from TradingView, a leading online trading provider. TradingView awards recognize brokers for delivering strong trading.
data.description: In London on January 30, 2026, ThinkMarkets, a prominent online trading provider, achieved the prestigious title of Europe's Best Forex and CFD broker for the year 2025. This recognition was bestowed by TradingView. The TradingView awards, held annually, highlight brokers on the TradingView platform who demonstrate exceptional performance in trading.

data.title: Traffic safety expert Amy Witherite comments on NTSB investigation into Waymo incidents with school buses in Dallas, raising concerns.
data.description: Amy Witherite, an expert in traffic safety and the founder of the Witherite Law Group, commented on the announcement by the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) to investigate incidents involving Waymo autonomous vehicles and school buses. This move signals an increase in federal oversight and raises concerns about the accuracy of Waymo's claims that previous safety issues had been addressed. The initiation of this second federal investigation indicates a serious development in ensuring public safety as autonomous vehicle technologies continue to evolve.

data.title: Global AI in supply chain market to reach $50.41 billion by 2032 at a CAGR of 20.2%, up from $13.93 billion in 2025.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, on January 30, 2026, it was reported that the global AI in supply chain market is expected to increase from USD 13.93 billion in 2025 to USD 50.41 billion by 2032, representing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 20.2%. This data comes from a report by MarketsandMarkets™. The report includes insights from 150 market data tables and 60 figures spread across 290 pages.

data.title: Inventor creates new tool, PITCH FINDER, to determine roof pitch/angle accurately for HVAC techs.
data.description: A resident of Fresno, California, who works as an HVAC technician, recently shared details about his innovative invention called the PITCH FINDER during an interview in Pittsburgh on January 30, 2026. The inventor explained that he created the device to assist in determining the rise and run distances for a roof in a user-friendly and efficient manner. The PITCH FINDER, designed with simplicity and accuracy in mind, aims to enhance precision while eliminating errors commonly encountered in such measurements.
